content.full_text:
State of California

Franchise Tax Board

Memorandum

To : Michael Bennett, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Date : November 7, 1977

File No.:

From : Robert C. Summers

Subject : Changes in Law and Regulations

In my testimony at the hearing on FTB Audits held November 3, I did not
include our recommendations on changes in the law and regulations
because of the time constraints. Attached are these recommendations
for your consideration.

We will be developing additional information relating to comments made
at the hearing and will subsequently communicate this to you.

Chief, Political Reform Audit Division
Att.
cc: Robert M. Stern
FPPC

Martin Huff
Executive Officer, FTB

RECOMMENDATIONS

Although we have not completed a four-year audit cycle, we do have some
recommendations for changes in the law or for regulations, which have
not already been made. These are:

Campaigns/Regulations

1. The current recordkeeping requirements under Reg. 18401 are too
general. The committees don't know exactly what type of details
and documentation to maintain for contributions, expenditures,
mass mailings, etc. Perhaps the "Recordkeeping Guidelines for
Campaign Committees and Treasurers" should be added to the
regulation as a minimal recordkeeping requirements.

In addition, the recordkeeping requirements should require photo-
copies of contributions received and expenditures should be backed
up by canceled checks and another form of source documentation.

2. The FPPC should adopt a regulation to establish minimum records
required to be maintained with respect to mass mailings:

(a) A copy of each mass mailing.

(b) A record for each mass mailing indicating:

(1) date mailed

(2) number mailed

(3) postage amount

(4) postage method (bulk rate, meter, etc.)

(5) if bulk rate permit, the name and address of the
post office.

Law Changes

1. Raise the threshold for detailed reporting of contributions and
expenditures from $50 to $100 or over. This would simplify
reporting. Statistics can be developed to indicate what level
of reporting would be lost by this change.

2. Section 84300 - "Cash Contributions" should include cashier's checks
and money orders. These are virtually nontraceable
items.

3. Alternative to (2) above.

The recipient of a cashier's check should obtain documentation
to reveal the source of the funds.

4. Require some minimal audit of filers not now under the mandatory
criteria such as lobbyist employers and nonmandatory committees.

Lobbyists/Law

1. 86105 - Accounts
   86106 - Payment of Expenses
   86107 - Contents of Reports

These sections should be changed to allow the FPPC to prescribe
the detailed requirements by regulation. The regulations can be
periodically modified to meet the reality of the field. The
Commission should determine what it wants disclosed by the lobbyists,
consistent with the purposes of the Act, and draft the regulations
with the assistance of an accounting specialist.

From the standpoint of disclosure, it would seem that the law, as
written, requires substantial detail on who is lobbying and who is
paying for it, but not much emphasis on what or who is being
lobbied unless that individual received a gift or any exchange. The
Commission may want to consider requiring the lobbyists to report
lobbying contacts, such as New Jersey or Wisconsin, or more detailed
information on the subject matter lobbied.

Regulations

1. Amend Reg. 18615(a)(1) to require lobbyists to keep a record of
   their lobbyist activities such as administrative or legislative
   action influenced or attempted to influence.

2. The concept of recording and reporting "deposits" to the lobbyist
   account should be eliminated because it adds nothing to disclosure
   under the Act since the source of each payment received is already
   required to be disclosed on page 1 of the monthly report.

2

sections.question_synthetic: This document does not contain a formal legal question; it is internal correspondence regarding recommendations for changes to law and regulations related to political reform audits.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is internal FTB-to-FPPC correspondence, not an FPPC advice letter response to a requester. Document contains fragmented text regarding account recording recommendations but lacks clear question/conclusion sections typical of advice letters. The document appears partially damaged or poorly scanned, with incomplete sentences and unclear formatting in the latter portion.

embedding.summary: A memorandum from Robert C. Summers (Chief of Political Reform Audit Division at the Franchise Tax Board) to Michael Bennett (Executive Director of the FPPC) dated November 7, 1977, transmitting recommendations for changes in law and regulations that were not included in his testimony at a November 3 FTB Audits hearing due to time constraints.
